text: Using HTTPS is the first step to secure any website. It encrypts data between the user’s browser and the server, protecting sensitive information like passwords. Authentication ensures only authorized users can access certain areas, often requiring strong passwords or two-factor methods. Input validation is crucial—it checks user-submitted data for harmful content, such as SQL commands that could delete a database. Without these measures, common vulnerabilities like cross-site scripting (XSS) and data breaches can occur. Developers must regularly update software and test for weaknesses, as security requires constant attention in today’s digital world.
